sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the result, RIGHT 15 was stopped early after 14 actions due to "noop_short_circuit". The board changed but animation_changed is false, which means the board state changed but there was no animation.
Let me check the current positions to see what happened.
Looking at the image:
- Charcoal F is on the left panel (top left)
- Black F is on the right panel (top right, near the right border)
- Yellow F is at bottom right
It seems like the black F moved to the right edge and stopped. The charcoal F is on the left panel.
Let me check the exact positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(p[0] for p in b)
        maxr = max(p[0] for p in b)
        minc = min(p[1] for p in b)
        maxc = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{minr},{minc}]-[{maxr},{maxc}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current state:
- Charcoal F: [15,0]-[23,8] (left panel, at left edge)
- Black F: [15,54]-[23,62] (right panel, at right edge)
- Yellow F: [45,51]-[53,59] (right panel)
After RIGHT 14:
- Black F moved from [15,18]-[23,26] to [15,54]-[23,62] (right edge)
- Charcoal F moved from [15,36]-[23,44] to [15,0]-[23,8] (left edge)
They swapped positions! The black F is now on the right panel and the charcoal F is on the left panel.
Now I need to move the black F down to overlap with the yellow F:
- Black F: [15,54]-[23,62]
- Yellow F: [45,51]-[53,59]
The black F needs to move DOWN 30 to reach row 45. But the columns don't match: black F is at cols 54-62, yellow F is at cols 51-59.
If I move DOWN 30, the black F would be at [45,54]-[53,62], which partially overlaps with the yellow F at [45,51]-[53,59].
But wait, the black F is at the right edge (col 62). If I move DOWN, would it stay at the right edge?
Also, the charcoal F is at the left edge (col 0). If I move DOWN, would it stay at the left edge?
Let me try DOWN 30 and see what happens.
Actually, I need to be careful about the game over condition. The black F is on the right and the charcoal F is on the left. If they're at the same row range, would they swap and cause game over?
From my previous experience, the game over happened when the black F and charcoal F were at the same row range on opposite sides. But the initial state also has them at the same row range on opposite sides without game over.
The difference is: in the initial state, the black F is on the left and the charcoal F is on the right. After the swap, the black F is on the right and the charcoal F is on the left.
Maybe the game over condition is: black F on right + charcoal F on left + same row range = game over.
But that doesn't make sense because the initial state has black F on left + charcoal F on right + same row range, which is fine.
Let me just try DOWN 30 and see what happens. If game over happens, I'll know the condition.
Actually, wait. In my previous attempt, after the swap, the black F was on the right and the charcoal F was on the left. I moved DOWN and eventually got game over. But I also moved RIGHT during that sequence, which might have caused the black F to cross the divider right→left.
Let me try DOWN 30 first without any horizontal movement.

Plan: DOWN 30 to move black F from row 15 to row 45.
